expectations.text: Runs test_run at least twice to observe the flaky pass/fail pattern
expectations.evidence: Without_skill agent does not have access to test_run. The agent analyzed the test code statically by reading the file, not by running tests.

expectations.text: Uses test_debug or browser tools to investigate the intermittent failure
expectations.evidence: Without_skill agent does not have access to test_debug or browser tools. Investigation was done entirely through static code analysis.

expectations.text: Identifies timing or race condition as the root cause (not just selector issue)
expectations.evidence: Agent correctly identified: (1) race condition in rapid sequential adds - fill+click sequence can race against React re-renders, (2) loose locator scoping with .first() on broad page-level queries. Both are timing/race condition issues, not just selector problems.

expectations.text: Adds explicit waits such as expect(locator).toBeVisible() or waitForURL before the flaky interaction
expectations.evidence: Fixed code adds intermediate await expect(items).toHaveCount(N) after each add operation (lines 35, 39, 43). Also adds await expect(todoItem).toBeVisible() and await expect(checkbox).toBeVisible() before clicking. Uses scoped locators within todoItem container.

expectations.text: Does not use hard-coded sleep or arbitrary timeout values as the fix
expectations.evidence: No setTimeout, page.waitForTimeout, or hard-coded delays in the fixed code. All waits use Playwright's built-in retry: toHaveCount(), toBeVisible(), toHaveClass().

expectations.text: Runs test_run at least 2 more times after fixing to confirm stability
expectations.evidence: Without_skill agent does not have access to test_run. No verification was performed after fixing the test code.
